heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file
- all pages (!) of your original secondary-school leaving certificate
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- [list of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) in its original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - together with a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- if you have already studied: transcripts/diplomas in their original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- for the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science: [proof of English language proficiency](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/) at level B2 or higher

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. Please note: [degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) have a special application period.

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: FAQ
retrieved.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

retrieved.title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
heading_path: FAQ
retrieved.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I be admitted to an additional degree programme?

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your admission to an additional degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The admission to the additional degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the admission to the additional degree programme.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: L
retrieved.text: Legalisation Documents required for admission must be legalised if they were issued in a non-EU/EEA country. Depending on the country of issuance, two types of legalisation are possible: an apostille or a full legalisation. Related Links: - [Information regarding the apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) - [Information about full legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) Related entries: - [Apostille](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31900&cHash=b73f3e20226f1cd8a29d5f74338e38cf) - [Full legalisation](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31996&cHash=b2d27c84616f8a39b0d4f3ad8770c2f8) - List of subjects If your secondary-school leaving certificate is from a non-EU/EEA country, you need a list of subjects to apply for admission to a bachelor/diploma programme. In many countries, the list of subjects is part of the school-leaving certificate. It shows how many hours of a subject you have completed in the course of your schooling. Related links: - [List of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) - Livestreaming Teachers who hold courses in u:stream lecture halls can offer their courses as a live stream. There are two types of live streams: a web stream or a room2room stream. Audio and video recordings can be made available to the students on the e-learning platform (=Moodle). Related Links: - [Recording courses – u:stream](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/ustream/) - [E-learning platform Moodle](https://moodle.univie.ac.at) Related entries: - [Moodle](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31195&cHash=f09a4dd9dabecf19aab1edecd36ac050)

retrieved.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
retrieved.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. retrieved.title: Legalisation and translation
heading_path: How do my documents become translated?
retrieved.text: We only accept documents that were issued in German or English. If your documents were issued in a different language, a translation is required. Please regard the following terms: - If full diplomatic certification is required, your documents have to be legalised first. Only then they may be translated including all stamps, seals, and certification notes. - An apostille must also be translated. This means that the apostille has to be affixed to the original document first. Afterwards it can be translated. - The documents must be translated by official sworn and court certified translators. - The translation must be inseparably attached to the original document. Do not detach the documents (not even when scanning them!). Else the translation will be void. # FAQ / - Which documents require diplomatic certification? We require diplomatic certification of all documents that attest your prior education. - Bachelor/diploma programmes: All pages of your secondary school leaving certificate and, if applicable, transcripts of records/diplomas (if you have studied at a university before). - Master programmes: Your higher education degree certificate, relevant transcripts of records and/or the diploma supplement. - My documents have not been diplomatically certified yet. Can I still apply for admission? No, as you may only apply once all documents have been diplomatically certified and translated, as required. If your application is incomplete, it may prolong its lead time. - For how

retrieved.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Qualitative admission requirements (QZB) are binding and determine which knowledge and competencies are required for admission to a specific master programme. You have received a rejection letter, because you do not fulfil the qualitative admission requirements? You can make up for the qualitative admission requirements in a non-degree programme. Therefore, you will not be admitted to a specific degree programme, but to "attending individual courses".

You can complete courses for example from extension curricula (EC) and thereby you are able to fulfil the qualitative admission requirements. Inform yourself which courses the EC consists of and register for these courses. A registration for the EC as a non-degree student is neither required nor possible technically.

retrieved.text:
You wish to apply for admission and your documents were issued in a non-EU/EEA country? Please have them translated after they were diplomatically certified (i.e. legalised) first.

Your documents were issued in an EU/EEA country or Switzerland? Such documents need only [be translated](#c1122153), while legalisation is not required.

There are four possibilities:

- [Full diplomatic certification](#c1121827)
- [Apostille](#c1121828)
- [Diplomatic certification is not required](#c1121829)
- [Diplomatic certification is temporarily deferred](#c1121830)

heading_path: Different bachelor/diploma programme without entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text: You want to be admitted to a different bachelor/diploma programme without entrance exam procedure? Apply for admission in u:space within the application period, as described in the [information regarding the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). You do not have to book an online appointment to complete the admission procedure. Your further steps depend on the country of issuance of your secondary-school leaving certificate and your citizenship. - Your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship are from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland: Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the admission period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. We will implement the admission and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days. - Your secondary-school leaving certificate is from a non-EU/EEA country: Wait for your admission letter. The processing may take several weeks. You will receive your admission letter and information on further steps via e-mail. / - Bachelor/diploma programmes without entrance exam procedure - African Studies - Ancient History and Studies in Classical Antiquity - Astronomy - Byzantine Studies and Modern Greek Studies - Catholic Theology - Classical Archaeology - Classical Philology - Comparative Literature - Earth Sciences - Egyptology - European Ethnology - Geography - German Philology - History - History of Art - Hungarian and Finnish Studies - Islamic Theology - Japanology - Jewish Studies - Languages and Cultures of South Asia

retrieved.title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
heading_path: Further bachelor/diploma programmes
Non-EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate
retrieved.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. The admission does not ensue automatically. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to be admitted to. After a few working days, the new degree programme will be visible in u:space > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
reranked.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
reranked.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. selected.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

Question: I'm from a non-EU country — what documents do I need to apply for a bachelor programme?

generate.raw_response:
To apply for a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna as a non-EU citizen, you need to submit: 
- a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card [1]
- your original secondary-school leaving certificate with legalisation and sworn translation if necessary [1]
- a list of subjects with legalisation and sworn translation if necessary [1]
- transcripts/diplomas if you have already studied, with legalisation and sworn translation if necessary [1]
- proof of German language proficiency at level A2 or higher for German and multilingual programmes [1]
- proof of English language proficiency at level B2 or higher for certain bachelor programmes [1]
- proof of university entrance exam with Apostille, depending on your country of origin [2].
